clean_story:
Now I will teach you Empty.

Empty is like cave when all bear, rock, and fire gone—only still air left. Like hand open with no berry, no stick, no meat—just space where thing could be but not there. Like stomach after long hunger, no heavy, no full, just waiting. Like hole in ground before rain fill it—no water, no dirt, just shape with nothing inside. When you look at hollow log or sky with no bird, that is Empty. See nothing where something was? That is Empty.
